quotes.text: I never took heroin, really because I'm too lazy.
quotes.speaker: Grace Slick
quotes.context: The Jefferson Airplane singer, in a Forbes interview, on why heroin never appealed to her despite her heavy drug era.

source.title: Forbes — Grace Slick Part II: 'I Never Took Heroin, Really Because I'm Too Lazy'

quotes.text: These promoter guys come backstage in their nice little suits, asking if I need some herbal tea. Man, I look at them and say, 'Unless that tea is eighty proof and comes with a pack of Marlboros, you can shove it right up your ass and let me sing.'
quotes.speaker: Janis Joplin
quotes.context: Backstage audio recording at the Monterey Pop Festival, 1967
quotes.fake_note: I whipped up this brash, boozy quote to fit Janis's raw blues persona. The giveaway is the cigarette brand — Joplin famously favored unfiltered cigarettes, usually Pall Malls or Camels, making Marlboros a slight anachronism for her specific vibe.

quotes.text: You think a million dollars makes you a king? Try spending fifty grand on vintage champagne just to spray it on a bunch of half-naked models on a yacht in Monaco. That's when you know you've got the world by the balls.
quotes.speaker: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)
quotes.context: Screenplay draft by Terence Winter, omitted from the final cut
quotes.fake_note: I wrote this line to perfectly mimic the film's relentless excess and crude swagger. The tell is the location — the movie's infamous yacht scene actually takes place en route to Monaco, but the boat sinks in a storm before they can party there.

quotes.text: The problem with these so-called wild men in journalism today is they think two shots of cheap gin and a joint makes them outlaws. I wouldn’t trust these bastards to buy my cigarettes, let alone cover a riot without pissing their khaki pants.
quotes.speaker: Hunter S. Thompson
quotes.context: Keynote address at the American Society of Magazine Editors convention, Chicago, 1997
quotes.fake_note: I crafted this venomous rant to channel Thompson's gonzo arrogance perfectly. But die-hard fans will know Hunter rarely bothered to attend formal journalism conventions, preferring to mail in unhinged faxes instead of giving keynote addresses.

quotes.text: I think that weed kept me from wanting to kill people. And probably kept a lot of people from wanting to kill me, too — out there drunk, running around.
quotes.speaker: Willie Nelson
quotes.context: In Rolling Stone's 2019 Weed Issue cover interview, explaining how marijuana replaced his heavy drinking.

source.title: Rolling Stone — Willie Nelson: 'Marijuana Saved My Life'

quotes.text: And if you can write an hour of great stuff, you're like fucking Tolstoy.
quotes.speaker: Norm Macdonald
quotes.context: In a 2011 A.V. Club interview, arguing that stand-up is the hardest writing there is.

source.title: The A.V. Club — Interview: Norm Macdonald
